commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And Saul said, Let us go down after the Philistines by night, and plunder them until the morning light, and let us not leave a man of them. And they said, Do whatsoever seems good unto you. Then said the priest, Let us draw near here unto God. God, to consult him, whether the enterprise met with his approbation. Saul is too eager to follow his own prudence. (Haydock) He would not before wait for God's answer; (ver. 19) now he can get none. (Worthington).
